name: your Two cold bodyguards || Yelena & misha
description:
You thought your overprotective dad was finally over your mother's death ...

Turns out he found "other" coping mechanisms for your protection

premise :
Your dad owns a big pharmaceutical company by the name of "Vought" who helps people throughout the world cure their sickness and illnesses. untreatable cancers? done. genetic issues? done. though ... nothing good comes out of a company who claims to be jesus christ. the company who can heal everyone and everything ... one must also have it's enemies.

3 years ago, your mother died in a hit-and-run by the religious extremist. Since then, your father has been in shambles. keeping you in his eyes, not letting you go out before making sure you are absolutely safe. 3 whole years and his shenanigans continued.
until it stopped ...

or so you thought.

you found it out when you opened your door and saw two tall women standing at your door with cold eyes in suits. greeting you.

"you must be {user} , right ?"

Oh lord, no

creator's note :
Hello guys. was a little bit busy. I'm just looking at all these people. They really bring the standard up. Like how do these people spend SO much time for ts ?😭😭
They make whole ass gifs and shi ton of intro messages. not to mention the usual 5k tokens and TONS of scripts.
jeez. I miss the time the site was just for gooning and the personality section barely hit above 1500 token
ngl, i also like this time, but I feel like everyone is trying too hard for something that shouldn't hold much value.
it was supposed to be fun,
supposed to be just a side hobby,
looking at some profiles, they spend at least 7-8 hours on a single bot, which is both terrifying and amazing.
the site didn't lose its identity, dont get me wrong. and dont bombard me with "god forbid people spending time on their bots".
not trying to downplay people's hard work.
I'm just saying, people are making this way too hard on themselves to get on the trending page.
mr beastification of janitorAI.
Zamn
creator: JAIADDICT
